samples.gvd:
module fsm (
    input IN,
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg MATCH
);

reg [2:0] state;
parameter S0 = 3'b000, S1 = 3'b001, S2 = 3'b010, S3 = 3'b011, S4 = 3'b100;

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        state <= S0;
        MATCH <= 1'b0;
    end
    else begin
        case (state)
            S0: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S1: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    state <= S0;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= S2;
                end
            end
            S2: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    state <= S3;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S3: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    state <= S4;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= S2;
                end
            end
            S4: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    state <= S0;
                    MATCH <= 1'b1;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= S2;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                state <= S0;
            end
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
```xml
<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects the sequence '10011' in the input signal IN. Outputs MATCH=1 when the sequence is detected, and MATCH=0 otherwise.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="S0" value="0000">State S0.</state>
        <state name="S1" value="0001">State S1.</state>
        <state name="S2" value="0010">State S2.</state>
        <state name="S3" value="0011">State S3.</state>
        <state name="S4" value="0100">State S4.</state>
        <state name="S5" value="0101">State S5.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="current_state" width="4" initial_value="0000">Current state of the FSM.</register>
        <wire id="next_state" width="4">Next state of the FSM.</wire>
        <assign next_state[0] = (current_state == 4'b0000 && IN == 1'b1) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
               next_state[1] = (current_state == 4'b0001 && IN == 1'b0) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
               next_state[2] = (current_state == 4'b0010 && IN == 1'b0) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
               next_state[3] = (current_state == 4'b0011 && IN == 1'b1) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
               current_state = (RST) ? 4'b0000 : next_state;
               MATCH = (current_state == 4'b0101) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
        </assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Sequential logic for detecting the sequence '10011' in the input signal IN. On each rising edge of CLK or reset, the FSM transitions between states based on the input signal. The output MATCH indicates whether the sequence has been detected.</implementation>
</module>
```
